objective: Re-evaluate whether Codex auth inspection should block scheduled execution when workspace policy drifts.

context_snapshot.topic_summary: The topic tracks how the backend should validate Codex session ownership before using a stored session for repeat execution.

current_best_hypotheses.title: Fail closed on workspace drift
current_best_hypotheses.summary: Scheduled execution must stop when session inspection cannot confirm the configured workspace root.

challenger_targets.title: Fail closed on workspace drift
challenger_targets.summary: Test whether a lighter warning-only path could preserve safety without blocking the queue.

active_conflicts.summary: Docs describe both session continuity signals and principal verification, but runtime handling must privilege verified identity.

open_questions: Can a stale but still refreshable session skip renewal if account/read succeeds?
Should healthcheck jobs and run.execute jobs share the same retry envelope?

queued_user_inputs.summary: The system should distinguish recoverable session age from true ownership ambiguity.
